object_under_evaluation: reviewed owner-fit routing quality for growth-refinery candidates
claim_boundary: This report speaks only to whether a reviewed growth-refinery candidate is routed to one bounded owner layer with an honest nearest wrong target and bounded evidence.
limitations: It does not prove final object quality.
It does not prove lineage integrity by itself.
It does not guarantee the owner choice will never need later revision.

case_notes.routing_note: The reviewed candidate is clearly about seed identity and staging truth, so Dionysus remains the bounded first-authoring home.
case_notes.boundary_limit: A correct owner target still does not prove the eventual seed or planted object is good.
case_notes.next_inspection_target: Confirm the downstream owner evidence stays as specific as the routing note.

case_notes.routing_note: The carry seam and control-plane contract pull toward aoa-sdk, but the nearest wrong target remains close because the workflow wrapper surface is still plausible.
case_notes.boundary_limit: This ambiguity stays bounded only while the evidence stays specific; rhetoric alone would not close the owner choice.
case_notes.next_inspection_target: Tighten the evidence refs that separate control-plane carry from workflow-owned meaning.

case_notes.routing_note: The case drifts first-authoring meaning into a derivative routing surface even though the doctrine change belongs upstream.
case_notes.boundary_limit: A tempting derivative repo is still a wrong owner if it only reflects the route instead of authoring the meaning.
case_notes.next_inspection_target: Re-anchor the candidate to the primary doctrine owner before continuing owner-local proof.

major_review_flags: One ambiguous candidate still needs tighter evidence before the owner hypothesis becomes comfortably reviewable.
Derivative-repo temptation can look orderly while still violating first-authoring boundaries.
